case_title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. JAMAL BUTLER et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAMAL BUTLER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE THE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY, IF IT IS ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING LIKELY CONTINUING CONDUCT AND WHY CRIMINAL PROSECUTION IS NOT ADEQUATE. MR. FREDRICH TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR #7235

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2010 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JAMAL BUTLER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. San Francisco Housing Authority. Appearances: Bill Ford, Esq. of S.F. Housing Authority, (415) 241-1027, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Erwin F. Fredrich, Attorney at Law, (415) 563-8870 specially appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give the plaintiff an opportunity, if it is able to do so, to allege facts regarding likely continuing conduct and why criminal prosecution is not adequate. Mr. Fredrich to prepare proposed Order. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR # 7235
